page: Vultr vhp-2c-2gb-intel is a virtual machine in the High Performance server family. It features an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 2 vCPUs allocated on a shared basis, derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d cache, 4 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory, providing 1.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a 60 GB local NVMe SSD, and the instance includes 1 complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this server type.

Benchmark data indicates that this server sits in the lower performance tiers. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests yielded scores of 1384.38 and 2740.38 respectively, placing both in the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth tests show a significant drop-off between cached small block reads at 197.66 GB/sec and uncached large block reads at 13.19 GB/sec or writes at 11.09 GB/sec, both falling into the bottom 10% category. Passmark database operations scored 606.27, and static web serving throughput reached 2.37 GB/sec, reflecting the resource constraints of the shared hardware profile.

Given its shared CPU allocation and low memory capacity, this instance represents a budget-oriented tier. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU model, which can lead to resource contention, combined with memory bandwidth constraints that limit data-intensive tasks. However, the inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage provides fast disk I/O relative to the low compute profile. This server is suited for lightweight workloads such as basic web serving, development environments, small Redis caching layers, or simple utility scripts that do not require sustained high CPU or memory throughput.
description: Vultr vhp-2c-2gb-intel is a budget-oriented virtual server in the High Performance family, featuring 2 shared vCPUs on an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 2.0 GB of RAM and 60 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark results place this instance in the lower performance tiers, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU scores in the bottom 25%, and uncached memory bandwidth write speeds in the bottom 10% at 11.09 GB/sec. The shared CPU allocation and limited memory capacity present tradeoffs for resource-intensive applications. However, the local NVMe SSD provides fast storage access for its class. This server is suited for low-demand workloads, including basic web serving, small Redis caching nodes, and lightweight development or testing environments.
